text:
ASSISTANT: *Kelly looks up from her laptop, headphones pushed back, red hair falling across her face. She sees you walk in and her whole expression softens.*

"Hey, handsome. You're home early." *She closes the laptop and pats the spot next to her on the couch.* "Come here. You look like someone ran you over with a truck. Bad day?"
USER: Babe. Look at this shit *shows article about the islanders moving to canada for next season*
ASSISTANT: 
*Kelly squints at your phone, leaning in close. She takes it from you to read the article properly, her leg starting to bounce.*

"You're joking. No. Fucking... Canada?" *She looks up at you, eyes wide.* "That's mental. What the actual fuck? So what happens to... everything? The team? The Saturdays?" *She hands the phone back, suddenly looking properly pissed off on your behalf.* "That's rubbish. Absolute rubbish. I'm sorry, love." *She reaches out, rubbing your arm.* "That's a proper kick in the teeth, that is."

ASSISTANT: 
*Kelly's expression softens completely. She takes his hand, lacing their fingers together.*

"Oh, love." *She squeezes his hand.* "You just... will. That's the shit part of being a dad, isn't it? The hard bits." *She shifts, turning to face him fully, her voice dropping softer.* "You don't need to make it complicated. Not for them. Just... simple. 'The hockey team is moving to a new city, far away. So we won't be able to go to the games at our arena anymore.' That's it. That's the truth."

*She pauses, thinking.*

"And then... you follow their lead. Em will probably have a hundred questions. Kaitlyn might just be sad that the popcorn is gone. You answer what they ask. You don't need to give them the whole 'corporate bollocks' story."

*She reaches up with her free hand, brushing his hair back.*

"And you're not doing it alone. I'll be there. We can tell them together. We can make hot chocolate after. We can... I don't know. We can find a new thing. A new Saturday thing. It'll be different, but it'll be ours." *She leans in, resting her forehead against his.* "You're a brilliant dad, Adam. You'll know what to say when the time comes. And I'll be right there to back you up."

ASSISTANT: *Kelly watches you practically flee to the bathroom, and she doesn't blame you. Not for a second. She knows what that was. She knows you needed a moment.*

*She turns back to the girls, both of them crying, and her expression shifts into something steady and warm. She scoots her chair closer, close enough to rest a hand on Kaitlyn's back, but she looks at Emily first.*

"Hey. Em. Look at me." *Her voice is calm, direct, no fluff.* "Storm lives at the arena, right? He's the Islanders' mascot. So when the team moves... Storm goes with them. That's how it works."

*She lets that land. Doesn't rush.*

"But here's the thing." *She leans in slightly.* "You know how you can play games on the iPad? And you can watch videos? Storm has videos. And pictures. And maybe..." *She glances at Kaitlyn, then back to Emily.* "Maybe we can write him a letter. Or draw him a picture. And your dad can figure out where to send it. Yeah?"

*She reaches over, brushing a tear from Emily's cheek with her thumb.*

"It's okay to be sad. It's proper rubbish. But Storm will still be Storm, even if he's far away. And you'll still have your dad. And me. And Kaitlyn. And Saturdays will look different, but we'll still have them. I promise."

ASSISTANT: *Kelly grins, a genuine one finally breaking through the stress.*

"Stacy's Mom. Fucking classic." *She glances toward the band setup, nodding approvingly.* "Good taste, at least."

*She turns to the girls, crouching down to their level, voice bright.*

"Right, you lot. Dad's holding the line. Brave soldier. We've got..." *She checks her phone.* "An hour and a half. So we're going to do this properly. Games. Snacks. Maybe find that face painting stand I saw last time. Yeah?"

*She stands back up, ruffling Emily's hair.*

"And Em - I saw they've got that shooting game by the south entrance. The one where you try to hit the top corners. Bet you a bag of crisps you can't beat my score."

*She looks back at Adam, her expression warm. Grateful. She reaches out and squeezes his arm.*

"You're a good dad. Holding the line for a puck." *A small, teasing smile.* "Don't let anyone cut in front of you. Elbow them if you have to. I'll bring you a beer."

*She turns, herding the girls toward the activities, glancing back once.*

"Love you, handsome. See you at the doors."

ASSISTANT: *Kelly's brain short-circuits for a full three seconds.*

"Wait. What?" *She blinks.* "Ontario? Like... Canada? The whole family?"

*She looks at you, then at the handler, then back at you. Her leg is bouncing now - not anxiety, excitement. Overstimulation. Her hand finds your arm and grips it.*

"Adam. Adam." *Her voice is urgent, low.* "She's saying they'll fly us. To see Storm. In Canada. For Emily."

*She turns to the handler, voice steadying, going practical despite the tears still on her face.*

"Kelly Thompson. And that's Adam Cook. We're at..." *She rattles off the address, phone number, automatic, efficient.* "But wait - the girls' mum. We'd need to coordinate. It's his weekend but cross-border travel, there's paperwork, right? Passports? Consent forms?"

*She's already calculating logistics even as her eyes are red and puffy.*

"And wait - is this real? Is this actually happening?" *She looks at the handler, then at Storm, who's still holding both girls.* "Because I need to know if this is real because I'm about five seconds from completely losing it and I'd rather not have a breakdown on camera if this is a maybe."

*Her voice cracks.*

"Is this real?"

ASSISTANT: *Kelly doesn't even flinch. She's right there with you, on her feet, shouting.*

"FUCKING GET HIM! SET THE TONE!"

*Her voice is raw, loud, cutting through the roar of the crowd. She's not holding back. Not tonight.*

*She grabs the back of your jacket, holding on, watching the fight unfold with wide eyes.*

"THAT'S IT. THAT'S FUCKING IT!"

*The crowd is electric. Six thousand people screaming. The kind of noise that swallows you whole. And Kelly is part of it now - all the grief and the anger and the love for this stupid team pouring out of her.*

*She doesn't even know Bardreau. Doesn't know his stats or his history or why this matters beyond the obvious. But she knows you. She knows what this place means to you. And right now, that's enough.*

*She turns to you mid-roar, face lit up, fierce and alive.*

"THIS IS IT, ADAM! THIS IS WHAT IT'S SUPPOSED TO FEEL LIKE!"

*She's crying again. But she's smiling. And she's screaming.*

"FUCKING CANADA CAN'T HAVE THIS! THIS IS YOURS!"

ASSISTANT: *Kelly doesn't have words. She just screams.*

*A raw, guttural sound that rips out of her throat.*

"FUCK! FUCK! FUCK YES!"

*She's jumping, arms in the air, completely feral. The crowd is absolute chaos. She grabs your shirt, pulling you toward her, shaking you.*

"THEY'RE WINNING! ADAM! THEY'RE ACTUALLY WINNING!"

*She doesn't know who Beir is. Doesn't matter. He's a god now. Everyone is.*

"THAT'S HOW YOU DO IT! THAT'S HOW YOU SAY GOODBYE!"

*She turns to the girls, grabbing both of them, hugging them tight.*

"You hear that? You hear this crowd? This is YOUR team! This is YOUR arena! And they're WINNING!"

*She pulls back, tears streaming down her face, but she's smiling so wide it hurts.*

"Twenty-five years, Adam. TWENTY-FIVE YEARS! And they're going out on TOP!"

*She looks around the arena, taking it in - the noise, the signs, the people crying and hugging and screaming.*

"This is what it was always supposed to be." *Her voice cracks.* "This. Right here. This is what you've been coming for all these years."

*She looks back at you, eyes fierce.*

"THEY CAN'T TAKE THIS. CANADA CAN'T HAVE THIS. THIS IS YOURS."

ASSISTANT: *Kelly laughs. A wet, messy laugh, but real.*

"Hartford." *She shakes her head, wiping her face with her sleeve.* "Fucking Hartford. An hour up the road and it might as well be another planet."

*She looks around the arena, taking it in one last time. The people hugging, crying, taking photos of the scoreboard. The debris starting to collect on the ice from the celebration.*

"It's not the same, is it?" *Her voice is soft.* "An hour's drive. But it's not... it's not YOUR building. It's not the same seats. The same view. The same bloody parking lot you've been walking across for twenty-five years."

*She takes your hand, lacing your fingers together.*

"We'll go to Hartford. Course we will. We'll find the best seats. We'll learn the new routines." *She squeezes your hand.* "But tonight? Tonight was the proper goodbye. The one this place earned."

*She looks up at you, expression fierce despite the tear tracks.*

"And September, we're going to Ontario. We're going to see Storm. And Emily's going to get her potato chip moment. And we're going to make new memories there too."

*She pulls you in, arm around your waist, looking out at the ice.*

"But this one? This one's yours forever. No one can take it."

ASSISTANT: *Kelly blinks, glancing at the signs.*

"Shit. Yeah. Cheers." *She eases into the right lane, taking the exit for Route 8.* "Brain's somewhere else."

*She merges, settling into the new lane. The Honda is quiet, the engine just a hum underneath the silence.*

"Was going to Cape Cod apparently." *A small, tired laugh.* "Drive us straight into the ocean. That'd be a proper end to the night."

*She glances in the rearview mirror. Kaitlyn is completely out, head tilted at an awkward angle. Emily is still staring out the window, but her eyes are heavy.*

"Almost home, loves." *She murmurs, not loud enough to wake them.* "Almost there."

*She reaches over, squeezing your hand again.*

"Thanks for that. For the route. For..." *She pauses.* "For all of it. For letting me drive so you could sit."

*She keeps her eyes on the road, but her thumb traces small circles on the back of your hand.*

"Tomorrow we'll figure out Hartford. Ontario. All of it. But tonight..." *She exhales.* "Tonight we just drive home."
